threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you ask for a 'roll' and get a confused look instead of a bread roll. It’s a 'barm' or 'batch' around these parts, mate.
Caught a night bus last week, sat next to a bloke passionately explaining the merits of various fried chicken shops in Brixton. He swore by the one with the neon sign.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a guy bargaining with a Waschbär over a leftover kebab outside my window. That's when I knew this place has layers.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find park benches with built-in chess tables—perfect for spontaneous games between strangers, day or night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always tell who’s from here by how they order their burrito—locals ask for a side of salsa, not extra. It's the little things.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo on the 14 Mission sharing his wedding playlist with the whole bus. Only in San Francisco do you get wedding vibes mid-commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just pop into locals' bars and get a drink. But the best tapas spots are often hidden behind the busy streets, away from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they always ask for a tall glass of 'agua con gas' at the bars instead of just ordering a 'copa de agua'.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the bus' means just one. Try asking locals for a route—it's all about numbers and letters around here.
Caught a glimpse of a guy on the 47 bus singing opera at 3 AM. Almost made the late night ride feel like a concert on wheels.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw someone using an umbrella as a walking stick in Tempelhofer Feld—classic Neukölln ingenuity.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can spot a group of locals casually debating traffic laws over coffee while a raccoon rummages through their recycling. Only in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their unbothered acceptance of the slowest Muni bus on 16th—while the newcomers are still checking their apps every two minutes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing beats watching a Muni bus pull up just as the sky opens up. Everyone dashes out, but then it’s packed like a can of sardines, as usual.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just cafés and shops, but the real charm is in the hidden plazas where locals gather, especially at sunset.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still head to the Sagrada Família for a photo op, oblivious to the quieter gems in Sant Antoni. Classic sign of a newcomer.
